blocks.content: The Meaning of Survival (2)

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Then, of course, I'll go with Shirone.
Amy moved to the red team, high-fiving Shirone, Iruki, and Nade in turn before taking her place.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Tch, eager to stand out. That's why freshmen are no good.
Screamer didn't like that Shirone had been the first to make a move.
Even those who had been in the senior class for two or three years were cautious about choosing teams.
For Shirone to boldly raise the team's flag was both a disregard for hierarchy and a display of self-importance.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: At least that guy is a bit better.
Screamer looked at Dante.
Seeing him still deep in thought, it seemed that the top ranks understood each other.
Although he still didn't like him, letting emotions influence evaluations was a shortcut to failure.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Hey, let's join Fermi's side together. I know from last year. With Fermi, victory is guaranteed. Of course, I won't be easy either.
If he could bring Dante over, he could isolate Shirone. That would attract even more team members.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm.
Dante didn't even listen to Screamer.
Lost in thought, he moved with his companions.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Let's go. I've decided.
Screamer's face twisted.
Even Dante, said to be the kingdom's best in talent, was joining Shirone's team.
What the heck? Are they kids or what...
When Dante, followed by Canis and Arin, crossed the line to the red team, it was beyond absurd—it was infuriating.
Are they out of their minds? Do they even know what survival is? If they had just checked last year's results, they'd know.
Last year's survival evaluation had a ratio of 22 to 8 in favor of the red team.
In the end, the majority's choice was correct.
Naturally, Fermi was among the 22 and became the final survivor after enduring up to the fifth stage.
Annoying. I have to do this again.
Screamer shuddered.
The experiences in the Virtual Zone today would be extreme situations that wouldn't even appear in nightmares.
Although the difficulty was prepared up to the seventh stage, there was no need to go that far.
Only one person in the history of Alpheus Magic School had passed the seventh stage of survival.
Even that was just a rumor.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Fine, whatever. Be a sacrifice for my points. Let's go, Pony.
Screamer turned and headed to the blue team. Pony, who had been watching Shirone, followed him.
As more students chose their teams, Boyle made his decision after much deliberation and spoke to Pandora.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: I'm going to the blue team. You should choose quickly too. You don't want people saying you just tagged along.
Pandora tilted her head.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Blue? Do you have a reason for being confident?
In my own way. But I won't tell you. I don't want to influence your judgment.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: It's okay, tell me. I won't be swayed.
Finally, Boyle looked around and whispered in Pandora's ear.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: As you know, survival evaluates endurance. It's about patience, not skills.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: So?
You know, Fermi has been the final survivor for four consecutive years.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: I get what you mean. But Shirone isn't easy either. He's an Unlocker. His mental strength must be considerable.
Boyle sighed regretfully.
Why was the difficulty of the survival evaluation set so high?
If even one person could endure, the competition wouldn't exist in the first place.
If someone could definitely pass the seventh stage, everyone would join that person, and the team balance would be 30 to 0.
So, whether it's Shirone or Fermi, they would both be eliminated.
Therefore, Fermi, who had taken the survival test for over four years, was much more advantageous.
The fact that students above Class Two were heading to the blue team without much doubt also supported Boyle's thinking.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Well, it's your decision. Anyway, I'm going to Fermi's side.
Boyle didn't share his thoughts.
It was a value judgment. Sharing information out of camaraderie was fine, but trying to persuade would crack the alliance.
Pandora watched Boyle join the blue team.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: She was also conflicted, but she had seen Shirone's skills with her own eyes.
Most of Class Three being on the red team probably had similar thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Well, it's either this or that.
Pandora followed Boyle to the blue team.
Even in the ruthless senior class, she realized the importance of allies.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: If it's 50%, let's live or die together.
By the time Pandora chose the blue team, the teams' outlines were becoming clear.
Students who hadn't scored high in evaluations were being extremely cautious until the end.
One of them was Maya.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Hmm, I guess...
Maya turned towards Fermi's side.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Hey, are you really coming here?
Collie sternly warned.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Screamer, no external pressure in team selection.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: I was just asking.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: That's a warning. One more and you're out.
Tch!
Screamer clicked his tongue and turned away.
But this was also part of his calculation.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Maya was worth pushing away with one warning.
He had suffered significant point losses by being in the same group as Maya in strategy and tactics.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: If they were on the same team, the point difference would remain, and he couldn't avoid being in the same group in strategy and tactics.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: But if the teams were divided and he won, he could escape from Maya with a 40-point gap.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Let's split here. You go to Class Three, and I'll go to Class One.
Feeling dejected, Maya moved to the red team.
Even if it wasn't external pressure, Screamer's hostile attitude made her unwilling to be on the same team.
She approached Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Can I join?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course. The more, the better. And it's not me accepting you; you chose our team.
Iruki and Nade stepped aside, making room for her.
It was a strategic decision to give her a sense of belonging, as well as being angry at Screamer's pettiness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Endurance is influenced by psychology. The more who can hold out longer, the better.
In the end, those with strong endurance would remain, but being with teammates was a powerful weapon in itself.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'll join this side too.
Dorothy, with sleepy eyes, joined the red team.
Then Luman followed, and the timid Suabi cautiously moved over, watching the situation.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Oh, this is unexpected.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Luman, who didn't like Shirone, choosing the red team likely meant Dorothy influenced his decision.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Dorothy was almost the only person Luman was somewhat close to, but Suabi, who was indecisive like she had decision paralysis, joining immediately was worth reconsidering.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Some in Class Two have considerable trust in Dorothy's judgment. So... is Iruki right about the brainwave?
With everyone having chosen their teams, only Aider remained.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Hmm, which side should I join to be seen as making the right choice?
Shirone was looking at him with a kind smile.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Feeling a sense of trust as a team, Aider also smiled and ran over.
Hmm! Hmm!
At that moment, Screamer covered his mouth and cleared his throat.
With one more warning, he'd be out, so he had to signal with a sound.
Fortunately, it seemed effective as Aider turned his head.
With Collie's sharp eyes watching, Screamer pretended to be indifferent, stalling for time.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Screamer...
Screamer was the one who bossed Aider around the most in the senior class.
But he also took care of him a lot, making him someone Aider couldn't afford to offend.
Changing his mind, Aider bowed to everyone on the red team and then ran to the blue team.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Teacher! I'm sorry! I'll change teams.
Collie sighed.
But he couldn't give a warning based on suspicion alone, so he concluded the procedure.
Coincidentally, the red and blue teams ended up with exactly 15 members each.
Hmm, a close match. It's been a while.
No, it was the first time since Fermi had excelled in the survival test.
Shirone's presence is that significant.
The advanced class and the senior class were entirely different worlds, but the title of the kingdom's top prospect was powerful.
The fact that he had earned the title by defeating the former strongest, Dante, also played a part.
Now that the teams are decided, enter the Virtual Zone.
As the 30 students moved to the center of the Virtual Zone, Collie followed and explained.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Listen carefully. This is a matter of life and death.
Class Three students, experiencing the survival test for the first time, looked particularly tense.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: The Virtual Zone is not the second training ground. It doesn't transmit information through anti-magic. The runes engraved on the floor use sophisticated magical engineering to inject specific sensations into your Spirit Zone as if they were real.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: If the body is under the control of the mind, it's not strange for someone trapped in a room-temperature freezer to freeze to death. This time, however, it was a method of directly injecting information into the extremely sensitive mental state of the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Even though it's virtual, it can still strain your mind. Once you enter the survival test, your brain will go into a semi-conscious state due to shock. Therefore, it's impossible to consciously exit the Virtual Zone.
A dark shadow fell over the faces of Class Three.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Especially from the fourth stage onwards, it becomes really dangerous, so the teachers will monitor your condition through vision. But remember, since this is an official Red Line evaluation, they won't be able to intervene to save you even in dangerous situations.
Collie held up two fingers.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: There are two ways to exit the Virtual Zone. First, deactivate your Spirit Zone. Second, if deactivation is impossible, shout 'Toako.' Remember this well. It's 'Toako.'
'Toako' is a word with no meaning, created as a code for the survival test.
Using meaningful words could lead to misuse during the test.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Teachers will not respond to any words other than 'Toako.' Whether you say 'Help me,' 'I give up,' or 'The Virtual Zone is malfunctioning,' they won't be able to save you.
All the senior class students showed determined expressions.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: What I want to emphasize is, do your best but don't be stubborn. This is just a rehearsal. Also, if you feel any abnormalities after exiting the Virtual Zone, report it immediately to receive mental treatment. Understood?
Yes!
The students' voices echoed through the mountains surrounding the training ground.
